title: oxytropis campestris subsp. gracilis
intro: Oxytropis campestris subsp. gracilis, also known as a type of lupine or lupin, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Oxytropis campestris subsp. gracilis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of the world.
